Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-29-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel J. McQueen, Esq. of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton, LLP; T:213.617.4183, for defendant/moving party, on Court Call;
Daniel J. Palay, Esq. of Palay Hefelfinger, APC, T:805.628.8220, for plaintiff/opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court takes DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings under submission.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Defendant Dignity Health'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings [Part 2 - For Entry Of The Tentative Ruling Only.]
Off Calendar Mar-19-2018 Off Calendar

Defendant Dignity Health Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings (Added To Calendar For The Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Continued To Mar-19-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Palay, Esq. 805-628-8220 of Palay Hefelfinger APC for Plaintiff Marina Rasnow-Hill MD through Court Call.
S. Edward Slabach, Esq. 916-646-1400 of Young Minney & Corr LLP for Defendant DIGNITY HEALTH through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views. Both sides Argue. The Court rejects the argument of Dr. Rasnow-Hill's "meeting of the minds" argument. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is continued to 3/19/18 at 9:30 am in dept. 302. Plaintiff's Counsel to submit further supplemental briefing on 2/28/18 only on the sole issue raised by the Court re: the arbitration act and it's the enforceability on the arbitration agreement. Dr. Hill is not allowed to raise any new argument. Defendant's Counsel to submit further opposition on 3/8/18. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Continued To Feb-21-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel McQueen, Esq. of Sheppard Mullins Richter & Hampton LLP, T:213.617.4183
Daniel J. Palay, Esq. of Palay Hefelfinger, APC, T:805.628.8220, for plaintiff/opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings except as to the issue of "meeting of the minds." [See the Court's tentative ruling.]

The parties may file additional briefs by February 14, 2018. The hearing is CONTINUED to February 21, 2018 at 9:30 am in Department 302. If the Court finds there's no meeting of the minds the Court will deny petition.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
